notes: Conservative v0.x floors. The seed corpus is small (9 servers / 96 tools), so per-rule numbers are statistically noisy; only aggregate, micro-averaged floors are gated. minPrecision is the load-bearing false-flag guard. Floors are set by the one-false-flag budget: after a change lands and the measured state on the committed corpus is TP/FP, minPrecision is the largest 0.05-multiple <= TP/(TP+FP+1), so the gate tolerates exactly one noise FP at the current positive count before failing. Floors only ratchet up as the corpus grows; a downward move is a ratified exception with a written reason. Current basis (2026-07-15, corpus = 9 servers / 96 tools): 12 TP / 0 FP / 3 FN => precision 100.0%, recall 80.0%, F0.5 95.2%; budget 12/(12+0+1)=92.3% => minPrecision 0.90. Floors are held, not ratcheted: three positives are synthetic regression guards (behavioral-inference provenance), so the number does not yet justify a higher floor. Recompute at every corpus expansion.
